$schema_note: Analyzer rules for static review of distributed BI pipelines. Rules REASON and emit verdicts; they do not execute or auto-fix. Each rule names the anti-pattern it detects. IDs are stable (references/id-conventions.md). Some rules reuse Python-layer correctness concepts (PY-*).

rules.name: Scaling out when single-node would do
rules.signal: Distributed engine chosen without a post-pruning size estimate showing it exceeds one machine.
rules.why: Distribution adds shuffle, scheduling, and cluster cost; single-node is cheaper when data fits.
rules.verdict_prompt: Was post-pruning working-set size estimated and shown to exceed a single node?

rules.name: coalesce(1) to force one file
rules.signal: coalesce(1) (or repartition(1)) before a write to produce a single file.
rules.why: Funnels all data through one core, killing parallelism and risking OOM.
rules.verdict_prompt: Is output file count controlled by right-sizing/compaction rather than coalesce(1)?

rules.name: Layout serves load step not query step
rules.signal: On-disk partitioning not aligned to the columns BI queries filter/join on.
rules.why: Misaligned partitioning forfeits pruning and forces repeated shuffles.
rules.verdict_prompt: Does the partition column match the dominant downstream filter/join?

rules.name: Unjustified wide transform
rules.signal: Join/groupby/distinct/sort with no prior filter, projection, broadcast, or pre-aggregation.
rules.why: Every shuffle is costly; many are avoidable.
rules.verdict_prompt: Is each wide step justified, with filtering/projection/broadcast applied first?

rules.name: collect()/toPandas() on a large frame
rules.signal: Distributed result pulled to the driver without a sized, bounded limit.
rules.why: Collapses cluster-scale data into one process; OOMs the driver.
rules.verdict_prompt: Is the collected result small and bounded, or should this be a distributed op?

rules.name: Shuffle join where broadcast fits
rules.signal: A large table is shuffle-joined to a small dimension that would fit a broadcast.
rules.why: Broadcasting the small side avoids shuffling the large side entirely.
rules.verdict_prompt: Is the small side (post-pruning) broadcast rather than shuffle-joined?

rules.name: Known skew ignored
rules.signal: A hot join/group key creates a straggler with no AQE/broadcast/salt mitigation.
rules.why: One skewed key makes a single task dominate the whole job.
rules.verdict_prompt: Is skew detected and mitigated (AQE skew-join, broadcast, or salting)?

rules.name: Row-at-a-time UDF for engine-expressible logic
rules.signal: Python UDF used where a built-in/column expression exists.
rules.why: UDFs are opaque to the optimizer and add serialization overhead.
rules.verdict_prompt: Can the UDF be replaced by built-in functions or a vectorized UDF?

rules.name: Read without pruning
rules.signal: Filters not pushed down; plan shows full scan despite a selective filter.
rules.why: Reading everything is usually the single biggest avoidable cost.
rules.verdict_prompt: Does the plan confirm partition/column pruning for the filter?

rules.name: Small-files problem
rules.signal: Output (or input) is thousands of tiny files from over-partitioning/micro-batches.
rules.why: Listing/opening many tiny files dominates over reading data.
rules.verdict_prompt: Are output files right-sized/compacted (not per-sliver)?

rules.name: Fan-out at scale (sum after one-to-many join)
rules.signal: An additive measure summed after a non-unique-key join.
rules.why: Fan-out double-counts and explodes shuffle volume.
rules.verdict_prompt: Was the many-side pre-aggregated to the join grain first?

rules.name: Distinct-count method undisclosed
rules.signal: Distinct count not labeled exact vs approximate, or approximate where exact required.
rules.why: Approximate counts carry error that must be disclosed; exact counts are costly.
rules.verdict_prompt: Is each distinct count labeled exact/approximate with error bound, and correct for its use?

rules.name: Non-additive measure summed at scale
rules.signal: A ratio/percentage/average/distinct count summed across groups.
rules.why: Non-additive measures are wrong when summed and defeat partial aggregation.
rules.verdict_prompt: Is each summed measure additive across the group dimensions?

rules.name: Indiscriminate caching
rules.signal: Frames cached that are not reused across multiple actions; no unpersist.
rules.why: Cache competes with shuffle memory and causes spill.
rules.verdict_prompt: Is each cache justified by multi-use and released when done?

rules.name: Append-only (non-idempotent) write
rules.signal: Job appends output for a slice it may reprocess; rerun would duplicate.
rules.why: Reruns and backfills duplicate data.
rules.verdict_prompt: Is the write partition-overwrite or merge-on-key, and does a double-run leave output unchanged?

rules.name: No late-data policy
rules.signal: Period totals computed once with no idempotent lookback for late arrivals.
rules.why: Late events are silently lost or double-counted.
rules.verdict_prompt: Is there an idempotent lookback window absorbing late data?

rules.name: Per-partition dedup instead of global
rules.signal: Deduplication applied within partitions only.
rules.why: Duplicate keys spanning partitions survive.
rules.verdict_prompt: Is dedup global on the grain key?

rules.name: Job success treated as reconciliation
rules.signal: No control totals; pipeline trusts that the job finished.
rules.why: Success is not correctness; only matching totals are evidence.
rules.verdict_prompt: Are distributed control totals reconciled to source/SQL?

rules.name: Sampling passed off as proof
rules.signal: A sample 'looks fine' substituted for a full-dataset aggregate check.
rules.why: A sample can miss systematic errors that totals catch.
rules.verdict_prompt: Are full-dataset aggregate checks present, with sampling only supporting?

rules.name: Bypassing gate or defining metrics in the job
rules.signal: Job self-approves, or encodes metric/semantic business logic.
rules.why: Readiness owns gating; retail-kpi owns metric meaning and DAX owns the measure. This layer reasons and hands off.
rules.verdict_prompt: Is the reconciliation record handed to the gate, with no metric/semantic logic encoded here?
